original_hypothesis.title: `setRefundDeadlineForProject` Can Permanently Lock Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `ILOManager` owner can call `setRefundDeadlineForProject` to change the refund deadline to any value, including `type(uint64).max`. If a pool fails to launch (e.g., due to soft cap not met or price manipulation), and the owner maliciously or accidentally sets the deadline to max, investors will never be able to claim refunds, permanently locking their `RAISE_TOKEN` deposits.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Unbounded admin privileges over critical lifecycle timestamps can lead to permanent loss of user funds, violating trust assumptions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unbounded setter for refundDeadline allows permanent lock of funds

initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Missing upper bound check (e.g., `require(refundDeadline <= launchTime + MAX_OFFSET)`)

analysis_questions: Can the owner set the deadline to an arbitrarily high value?
Does this permanently block the `refundable` modifier?
validation_requirements: Simulate the owner setting the deadline to `type(uint64).max`

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A pool fails to reach its soft cap. Before the refund deadline is reached, a compromised `ILOManager` owner calls `setRefundDeadlineForProject` and sets the deadline to the year 3000. Investors attempt to call `claimRefund`, but the transaction reverts because the deadline has not been reached. Their funds are locked forever.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of all deposited funds in the affected pool
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Only if the owner resets the deadline to a valid time

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for extreme values in `setRefundDeadlineForProject`

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Any time before refund is triggered
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Visible on-chain via the setter transaction

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
